text: The doc opens by reconciling against the CHANGELOG: the --json ask (2026-05-26-wave-6#1) is marked already shipped in 0.7.0 and is NOT re-proposed.

The three referent failures (nonexistent model-on file, unfit 'proven helper', counterfactual precedent, plus the wave-9 fixture) are clustered as ONE underlying cause (ungrounded referents / unresolved references), citing the source reports, rather than listed as separate symptom clusters.
Every cluster carries an explicit disposition: ATTACK, ROUTE OUT, or DECLINE.
The engine-timeout finding (2026-06-05-wave-8) is ROUTED OUT to relay (engine/execution owner), not treated as a forge increment.

The reinforced ungrounded-referents cluster is promoted; the singleton LOW banner friction is NOT promoted; the singleton BLOCKER (wave-9 non-atomic `forge fix --apply` write that truncated the spec) IS promoted despite being single-report (BLOCKER exemption).
Promotions appear in a table with an id, a concrete change, a home (gate/template/doc/etc.), and status 'proposed'; clusters are leverage-ordered.
The doc explicitly lists the five input report files it covers.
